title: Vast raises $500 million for commercial space station development
summary:
Commercial space station developer Vast has raised $500 million in its first significant outside investment round.

title: Jared Isaacman on rebuilding, Artemis and what he’s learned during his first months as NASA administrator
summary:
When Jared Isaacman was sworn in as NASA administrator Dec. 18, he hit the ground running — or, perhaps more accurately, hit the air flying. At a town hall the next day, he said he would visit all the agency’s field centers, a task he completed by late January. In some cases he showed up […]

title: Training for the εpsilon mission
summary: ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot began her training at the European Astronaut Centre in Cologne, Germany, where she studied spacecraft systems and crew operations — learning to think and act as an astronaut. Alongside this, she conditioned her body for spaceflight and prepared for the physical and operational demands of her mission.

title: ESA’s Mars orbiters watch solar superstorm hit the Red Planet
summary: What happens when a solar superstorm hits Mars? Thanks to the European Space Agency’s Mars orbiters, we now know: glitching spacecraft and a supercharged upper atmosphere.

title: Lunar dust model maps how charged grains stick to spacecraft
summary:
Tokyo, Japan (SPX) Mar 05, 2026


Researchers from Beijing Institute of Technology, China Academy of Space Technology, and the Chinese Academy of Sciences have developed a theoretical model that links electrostatic forces and contact mechanics to describe how low-velocity charged lunar dust particles either adhere to or escape from spacecraft surfaces within a surrounding plasma sheath. title: Danish Mani mission to chart lunar terrain in 3D
summary:
Berlin, Germany (SPX) Mar 05, 2026


The University of Copenhagen will lead Denmark's first lunar mission, a European Space Agency project that will map the Moon's surface in three dimensions to support future landings and base construction. 

The Mani satellite will orbit the Moon's north and south polar regions, acquiring high-resolution images that can be combined into detailed elevation models.

title: Ancient impact may explain moons contrasting sides
summary:
Tokyo, Japan (SPX) Mar 05, 2026


For decades scientists have puzzled over why the moons two hemispheres look so different despite sharing a common origin in the early solar system.

title: Lunar dust study links space weathering to changes in Moon ultraviolet brightness
summary:
Los Angeles CA (SPX) Mar 05, 2026


SAN ANTONIO - Southwest Research Institute (SwRI) scientists working with researchers at UT San Antonio have analyzed Apollo lunar soil samples to determine how space weathering alters the Moon's surface and affects its far-ultraviolet (FUV) reflectance.
